diagnosis: Check if another operation is running
Verify the lock ID from error message
Check who holds the lock and when it was acquired
resolution: Wait for ongoing operation to complete
If operation is stuck: terraform force-unlock <lock-id>
If using S3 backend: check DynamoDB table for lock entry

diagnosis: Check CloudTrail/Activity Log for manual changes
resolution: If Terraform state is correct: terraform apply to reconcile
If manual change is desired: terraform import or state manipulation
If state is corrupt: restore from backup
prevention: Set up drift detection in CI

symptoms: Error: Cycle detected in dependency graph
resolution: Use depends_on explicitly to break cycles
Refactor resources to remove circular dependencies
Split into separate terraform apply operations

steps: 1. Write the resource block in .tf file matching current config
3. terraform plan to verify no changes
4. Adjust .tf file if plan shows differences
tips: Use terraform show after import to see imported state
Import one resource at a time
Document imported resources for team awareness

state_management: Always use remote state backend (S3+DynamoDB, Azure Blob, GCS)
code_organization: Separate environments (dev/staging/prod) with workspaces or directories
security: Use vault/parameter store for sensitive values
